rulings.Case Title: ELVIRA B. JOHNSON, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS PERSONAL et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Dap, Inc., Joinder To Defendant Merlex Stucco, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Abate Entire Action Pending Joinder Of All Necessary Parties
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 16, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant DAP, Inc.¿s joinder in Merlex Stucco, Inc.¿s motion to abate entire action pending joinder of all necessary parties is denied. Heirs are necessary but not indispensable parties to a wrongful death action. (Ruttenberg v. Ruttenberg (1997) 53 Cal.App.4th 801.) All necessary parties were initially joined as plaintiffs when this action was filed. Jeff London and Brent Revok later voluntarily dismissed their claims with prejudice. Mr. London and Mr. Revok therefore will not be able to later bring a wrongful death action against defendant. (See, e.g., Boeken v. Philip Morris USA, Inc. (2010) 48 Cal.4th 788; Alpha Mechanical, Heating & Air Conditioning, Inc. v. Travelers Casualty & Surety Co. (2005) 133 Cal.App.4th 1319.) No abatement is required and this court maintains the power to adjudicate this action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CAROL HAYNES et al VS. BASF CORPORATION INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR IN
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 16, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Selman Breitman LLP¿s motion to be relieved as counsel for Vanderbilt Minerals LLC f.k.a. R.T. Vanderbilt Company, Inc. is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
